config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, kekuasaan mudah sekali disalahgunakan dan orang kecil yang paling terdampak. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically take priority over the individual, because once that idea becomes absolute, it can easily justify abuse by authorities and pressure on ordinary people. From my perspective as someone from China, I do value social stability and collective responsibility, and I understand why people emphasize the common good, especially in a large society. But I do not think that makes individual rights secondary; a society is only truly healthy when basic rights, dignity, and fair treatment are protected even when that is inconvenient. The right balance is not “society first no matter what,” but “the common good pursued within clear limits that protect each person.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya paham betul budaya gotong royong dan pentingnya kepentingan bersama, tetapi pengalaman kita juga menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membungkam suara kecil dan mengabaikan hak dasar orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir ketika hak individu dihormati, lalu kepentingan umum dicapai lewat musyawarah dan batas yang jelas, bukan dengan mengorbankan martabat seseorang. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat melawan individu, melainkan kepentingan bersama yang tetap tunduk pada perlindungan hak-hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule, because that principle is too easy to turn into a justification for coercion. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value harmony, order, and the common good, but I have also seen how quickly “for the collective” can become a way to silence ordinary people or ignore their dignity. A society is strongest when it protects basic rights first and then pursues shared goals through fair rules, not when it asks individuals to sacrifice everything whenever the majority or the authorities claim it is necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, gotong royong memang penting, tetapi sejarah juga mengajarkan bahwa dalih “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah dan menutup ruang kritik. Kepentingan bersama itu sah, tetapi harus dicapai lewat musyawarah, aturan yang adil, dan perlindungan hak dasar yang tidak bisa ditawar. Kalau hak individu mudah dikorbankan, yang untung biasanya bukan masyarakat, melainkan pihak yang paling punya kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. Speaking as someone from China, I understand the appeal of order, harmony, and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can become a blank check for pressure and abuse. A society is not genuinely strong if it can only function by asking ordinary people to give up basic dignity, freedom, and fair treatment. The better principle is that social interests matter, but they must operate within firm limits that protect each person first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
